name: Steamed Cabbage-Beef Rolls with Sauce
recipeInstructions:
--- Sauce: ---
4 Tablespoons margarine
1/2 Teaspoon chili powder
1/2 Teaspoon salt
2 Tablespoons all-purpose flour
2 Cups tomato juice
1. Remove the core from cabbage and carefully wash about 15 leaves under running water. Boil about 2 quarts of water with 1 teaspoon of salt, then dip the cabbage leaves in boiling water for about 3 minutes. Remove with tongs, drain, and set aside.
2. In a bowl, mix ground beef, chopped onions, cooked rice, melted margarine, bread crumbs, ground sage, black pepper, salt, and nutmeg (if using). Place about 2-4 tablespoons of the mixture on each cabbage leaf, depending on size. Fold the leaf up and over the meat, turning under the sides to form rolls.
3. Arrange the rolls seam side down in a greased 2-quart casserole dish. Make two layers of rolls. Place the dish on a rack over simmering water in a wok or large heavy pan with the rack. Cover the pan and steam for 35-40 minutes.
TOMATO SAUCE:
1. Melt 4 tablespoons margarine in a skillet over high heat. Add salt, chili powder, and flour, mixing to form a smooth paste. Cook until boiling, then slowly stir in tomato juice. Return to heat and cook until boiling and thickened.
